confirmed.title: theme-package ThemeToggle aria-label/title hardcoded (PT-BR literal, no translator)
confirmed.detail: const LABELS maps each theme to a hardcoded pt-BR string ('Tema: sistema (clique para claro)', 'Tema: claro (clique para escuro)', 'Tema: escuro (clique para sistema)') used at lines 92-93 as both aria-label and title. This is a distinct component from the ui/shell/Topbar theme toggle (round-1 ledger) — this one has NO translation hook at all and bakes in a single language. visible: title tooltip + aria-label (a11y).
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at packages-js/theme/src/ThemeToggle.tsx. Lines 7-11 define const LABELS as a Record<Theme,string> of hardcoded PT-BR literals ('Tema: sistema (clique para claro)', etc.), consumed verbatim at lines 92-93 as both aria-label and title on the toggle button. useTheme() (line 3/84) returns only {theme,setTheme} — there is NO translation hook anywhere in the component, so the strings are baked into a single language and are user-facing (visible title tooltip + screen-reader aria-label). Genuinely reproduces.

Not a duplicate: scanned the full R1-R6 ledger. No entry references the packages-js/theme/ package or ThemeToggle.tsx. The closest is the round-1 entry packages-js/ui/src/shell/Topbar.tsx:57 ('Theme toggle aria-label built from hardcoded English template'), but that is a distinct file/component in the ui/shell package (and English-template, not a no-hook PT-BR literal). This theme-package component is independent and never logged.

Not vendored: located in packages-js/theme/src/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. The icons and button are hand-authored in this file, not a shadcn primitive.

confirmed.title: a11y SkipLink default label hardcoded pt-BR
confirmed.detail: label default = 'Pular para o conteúdo principal'. The visible+focusable skip-link text falls back to a hardcoded pt-BR literal with no translation hook; English (or any other-locale) panels render Portuguese. visible link text.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/a11y/src/SkipLink.tsx:40: `label = 'Pular para o conteúdo principal'` is a hardcoded pt-BR default with no translation hook (no t()/useArqelTranslations). The label renders as visible, focusable skip-link text (line 74, `{label}`), so a non-pt-BR panel that mounts SkipLink without an explicit label prop displays Portuguese — a genuine user-facing i18n defect. Not vendored: the file is packages-js/a11y/src/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: the R1-R6 ledger contains zero entries for the packages-js/a11y package; all prior i18n findings are in ui, auth, ai, versioning, workflow, realtime, fields-js, fields-advanced, i18n, react, or PHP packages. Confidence is med rather than high only because the label is a consumer-overridable prop, so it triggers solely when the consumer omits the label on a non-pt-BR locale — but that framework-default-fallback pattern is exactly what the ledger consistently treats as a real defect.

confirmed.title: fields-js ImageInput: alt + visible button text hardcoded English, no translator

'Replace image' : 'Choose image'} (hardcoded visible button label). The component imports only cn and React hooks — no useArqelTranslations / translator hook — so neither string can be localized. This is a real, user-facing i18n/a11y defect in a first-party field renderer. Not vendored: the file is in fields-js, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: scanned the full R1-R6 ledger; ImageInput.tsx never appears. The fields-js entries in the ledger cover CurrencyInput, DateTimeInput, NumberInput, FileInput, MultiSelectInput and BelongsToInput — but not ImageInput. The sibling FileInput aria-label defect was accepted in R4, confirming this is a recognized defect class, yet ImageInput itself was never logged.

confirmed.title: KeyValueField ships hardcoded English 'Key'/'Value' column labels verbatim
confirmed.detail: protected string $keyLabel = 'Key'; and protected string $valueLabel = 'Value'; (lines 38, 40) are framework-hardcoded English defaults serialized verbatim in toArray() at lines 177-178 ('keyLabel' => $this->keyLabel, 'valueLabel' => $this->valueLabel). Unless the app developer calls ->keyLabel()/->valueLabel(), the column headers and aria-labels render 'Key'/'Value' in English regardless of locale. These are framework-hardcoded defaults, never passed through __(). Should default to a translatable key like __('arqel::fields.keyvalue.key') / '...value'.
confirmed.why_real: Verified in source: packages/fields-advanced/src/Types/KeyValueField.php lines 38/40 declare protected string $keyLabel = 'Key'; / $valueLabel = 'Value'; and getTypeSpecificProps() (lines 174-187) serializes them verbatim at lines 177-178 with no __() call. The setters keyLabel()/valueLabel() (lines 71-95) only override the English defaults if the app developer calls them; otherwise the framework default ships. End-to-end user-facing impact confirmed: the React consumer packages-js/fields-advanced/src/key-value/KeyValueInput.tsx (the docstring claiming it is 'future' is stale) renders props.keyLabel/valueLabel as visible column headers (lines 239-240), as input aria-labels (lines 255, 267), and inside an add-row aria-label (line 293). So with no override the headers and aria-labels render English 'Key'/'Value' regardless of locale. Ledger check (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json, rounds 1-6): no entry references KeyValueField.php or fields-advanced/src/Types/ at all; the only fields-advanced entry is MarkdownInput.tsx (round 4, JS aria-labels) which is unrelated. This is the same documented 'PHP $label serialized verbatim, never through __()' family the ledger keeps confirming (Field.php R1, Column.php R1, Action.php R1, Widget.php R2, NavigationItem.php R3, SelectField.php R6), but this specific field type is novel and uncovered. Not vendored: path is packages/fields-advanced/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: Form Tab::$label serialized verbatim — never passed through __()
confirmed.detail: toArray() emits 'label' => $this->label (line 76) exactly as supplied. Mirrors the already-fixed Field/Column/Widget label gap (ledger rounds 1-3) but the form Tab layout primitive was not swept. The developer-supplied tab label is shipped to the React form-tabs renderer with no translation pass, so a key/string passed to Tab::make($id, $label) is never resolved through trans(). App-must-key on the framework side: framework should wrap with __() like the other label-bearing toArray() serializers.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages/form/src/Layout/Tab.php:76: getTypeSpecificProps() emits 'label' => $this->label verbatim, with no __()/trans() pass anywhere in the class (constructor line 29, make() line 32 store the raw $label). The React FormTab renderer receives this label as visible tab text, so a developer-supplied key/string passed to Tab::make($id, $label) ships untranslated — identical to the already-fixed Field/Column/Action/Widget label gap. NOT vendored: this is framework code under packages/form, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. NOT a duplicate: the R1-R6 ledger covers the label-serialization family for Field (R1), Column (R1), Action (R1), Widget heading/description (R2), Resource label (R3), NavigationGroup/NavigationItem (R3), and SelectField option labels (R6), but contains NO entry for packages/form/src/Layout/Tab.php, FormTab, or any form Layout primitive. The form Tabs/Tab layout primitive was never swept. Genuine, novel, user-facing instance of the established label-translation-gap family.

confirmed.title: Form Section heading/description serialized verbatim — never through __()
confirmed.detail: toArray() emits 'heading' => $this->heading and 'description' => $this->description (lines 106-107) verbatim. Same untranslated-label family as Field/Column/Widget/Tab. Section::make($heading) and ->description() values reach the React form renderer with no trans() resolution. Framework should pass both through __() in toArray() for parity with the round-1/2/3 label fixes.
confirmed.why_real: Verified the code at packages/form/src/Layout/Section.php lines 105-114 (getTypeSpecificProps, which the finding loosely cites as 106): toArray emits 'heading' => $this->heading (line 106) and 'description' => $this->description (line 107) verbatim, with no __()/trans() resolution anywhere in the class. Section::make($heading) and ->description($description) accept raw strings that flow through to the React 'FormSection' component (component='FormSection', line 15) untranslated. This is genuinely user-facing: form Section headings/descriptions render in the admin form UI. It is framework PHP, NOT a vendored shadcn primitive (packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). It is the same untranslated-label family fixed for siblings in prior rounds — Field::$label (R1), Column::$label (R1), Action::$label (R1), Widget heading/description (R2, packages/widgets/src/Widget.php:238), NavigationItem/NavigationGroup label (R3), SelectField option labels (R6) — but the form Section component itself does NOT appear anywhere in the rounds 1-6 ledger. The nearest neighbor, Widget.php:238, is a different file/class. So it is novel and not a duplicate. Confidence high: code reproduces exactly as described, pattern is well-established as a confirmed defect class, and no ledger entry covers this file.

confirmed.title: fields-advanced Block::label() serialized verbatim in toArray()
confirmed.suggested_key: (app-supplied via Block::label(); framework should __() in Block::toArray)
confirmed.detail: toArray() emits 'label' => static::label() (line 78) — the block's display name in the 'Add block' menu — exactly as the subclass returns it, with no __() pass. Block labels reach the BlockEditor/builder React UI untranslated. Same untranslated-label family; the round-4 fields-advanced sweep only covered editor aria-labels, not this serialized block label.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/fields-advanced/src/Blocks/Block.php:78: the final toArray() emits 'label' => static::label() verbatim, with no __() pass, into the FieldSchema payload consumed by the React BuilderInput/BuilderField "Add block" menu. This is the block's user-facing display name, so a label set as a translation key or fixed English string is never localized.

NOT VENDORED: file is in packages/fields-advanced/src/Blocks/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

NOT A DUPLICATE: scanned the full R1-R6 ledger. This exact file/site is absent. The closest fields-advanced entry (round 4) covers MarkdownInput.tsx editor aria-labels — a different file and concern. The verbatim-untranslated-label family in the ledger covers different classes: Field.php:181 (R1), Column.php:382 (R1), Action.php:327 (R1), Widget.php:238 (R2), Resource getLabel/getPluralLabel (R3), NavigationGroup.php:141 (R3), NavigationItem.php:212 (R3), SelectField.php:177 (R6). Block.php is a novel site in this same accepted family.

REAL: shape is identical to sibling entries the team already accepted as defects — a user-supplied static label serialized verbatim through a toArray() React serializer without __(). The team fixed NavigationItem/NavigationGroup/Column/Widget on exactly this basis.

Confidence is med (not high) because Block is abstract with no concrete subclasses shipped in the framework, so the defect only manifests for user-defined block subclasses. However, this is the same situation as NavigationItem/Column (user-instantiated), which were accepted as real, so the precedent supports confirming.

confirmed.title: Wizard Step label (and English humanization fallback) serialized verbatim
confirmed.suggested_key: (app-supplied; framework should __() label and avoid English Str::title fallback)
confirmed.detail: toArray() emits 'label' => $this->getLabel() (line 138). getLabel() (lines 89-100) returns the explicit label verbatim OR, when none is set, a humanized step name built with Str::title() — an English-style Title-Case heuristic ('user_details' -> 'User Details'). Both the developer label and the auto-humanized fallback ship to WizardInput.tsx with no __() pass. Framework-hardcoded humanization is locale-blind; explicit labels are never translated.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED. packages/fields-advanced/src/Steps/Step.php:138 emits 'label' => $this->getLabel() in toArray() with no __() pass. getLabel() (lines 89-100) returns the developer-supplied label verbatim, OR — when none is set — a humanized fallback built via Str::of($name)->snake()->replace('_',' ')->title()->toString(), an English Title-Case heuristic ('user_details' -> 'User Details') that is locale-blind. This payload feeds WizardInput.tsx, where step.label renders as the visible step tab/title text (lines 491, 503) and inside accessible labels (lines 474 'Step N: {label}', 513 'Step N of M: {label}'). So both the explicit label and the auto-humanized fallback reach the UI untranslated — genuinely user-facing.

NOVEL: The ledger has no entry for packages/fields-advanced/src/Steps/Step.php or any Wizard step label. The only fields-advanced ledger rows are round-4 JS aria-label findings (MarkdownInput.tsx and other packages-js editor controls); none touch this PHP Step serializer. Rounds 1-6 do not cover it.

This is a distinct instance of the project's confirmed PHP-label-serialized-verbatim defect family (R1 Field::$label, Column::$label, Action::$label; R2 Widget heading/description; R3 Navigation*::label, Resource::getLabel; R6 SelectField option labels), which validates it as a real defect class rather than refuting it. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it lives in PHP under packages/fields-advanced, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*).

The component imports only from @arqel-dev/ui/form and 'react' (lines 14-21); there is no useTranslator/translate import and no localized source for this string. This is genuine user-facing UI chrome (screen-reader label on an interactive button), not a field-provided value. By contrast the placeholder (line 198) correctly derives from field.placeholder and the input aria-label (line 190) from field.label/field.name, so those are not defects — the finding's scoping is accurate.

Not vendored: the file lives under fields-advanced/src/tags/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*, so the vendored-primitive exclusion does not apply.

Not a duplicate: scanned the full R1-R6 ledger. The only fields-advanced entry (Round 4, MarkdownInput.tsx:430) explicitly enumerates its covered components as 'Repeater/Wizard/Builder/Markdown/RichText' — Tags is not among them, and TagsInput is a distinct file with a distinct line. The MultiSelectInput chip-remove entry (Round 4) is a different package (fields-js) and different file. No ledger entry references tags/TagsInput.tsx or 'Remove tag'. Novel, real, user-facing, non-vendored.

confirmed.title: Marketplace SubmitPluginRequest has no messages()/attributes() — :attribute renders raw snake_case English
confirmed.detail: SubmitPluginRequest defines rules() (lines 43-49: slug/composer_package/github_url/type/name/description) but declares neither messages() nor attributes(). Same validation-i18n gap fixed for ResourceController (ledger round 2) and ActionController (round 6): Laravel's default validator renders :attribute as the raw field name ('composer_package', 'github_url') and emits English default rule messages, with no localized attribute names. Plugin-submission form errors surface untranslated.
confirmed.why_real: Verified by reading packages/marketplace/src/Http/Requests/SubmitPluginRequest.php. The class extends FormRequest and defines rules() (lines 40-54) with snake_case fields composer_package, github_url, npm_package, plus type/name/description/slug, but declares NEITHER messages() NOR attributes(). Laravel's default validator therefore renders :attribute as the raw field name (e.g. 'composer package', 'github url') and emits English default rule messages, with no localized attribute names — reproducible, user-facing (plugin-submission form errors). This is the same validation-i18n family as ledger round 2 (ResourceController.php:763) and round 6 (ActionController.php:115), but those are different files in packages/core and packages/actions. A full scan of i18n-fixed-ledger.json shows NO entry for any file under packages/marketplace/src/; the only marketplace-related entry is apps/marketplace/resources/views/app.blade.php (round 6), an unrelated app-layer blade <html lang> issue. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it is a PHP FormRequest). Novel location, genuinely reproduces, user-facing, not in R1-R6 ledger.

confirmed.title: FormSection heading & description serialized verbatim — never passed through __()
confirmed.suggested_key: wrap Section heading/description in __()/Lang::get at serialization
confirmed.detail: Section::getTypeSpecificProps() emits 'heading' => $this->heading and 'description' => $this->description directly. These developer-supplied strings are rendered raw on the React side (packages-js/ui/src/form/FormSection.tsx:65/70 render {heading} verbatim), so a translation key or English literal set via ->heading('users.section.profile') is never resolved through __()/Lang. This is the exact same gap already fixed for Field::$label, Column::$label and Widget heading/description in the ledger, but FormSection was missed. Repro: define a Form with Section::make()->heading(__('app.profile'))->description('Profile details') under a non-English locale → the section header always shows the English/key text regardless of app locale.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against source. packages/form/src/Layout/Section.php:103-114 getTypeSpecificProps() emits 'heading' => $this->heading and 'description' => $this->description with no __()/Lang resolution. The React consumer packages-js/ui/src/form/FormSection.tsx renders these raw at line 27 ({config.heading}) and line 29 ({config.description}) — and notably does pass the collapse toggle through t() at line 39, proving heading/description are the only un-localized strings in that component. So a developer-supplied translation key or English literal set via ->heading()/->description() reaches the DOM verbatim regardless of app locale.

This is genuinely user-facing and matches the framework's own established i18n convention: the ledger already confirms the identical 'serialized verbatim, never through __()' pattern as a real defect for Field::$label (R1), Column::$label (R1), and most directly Widget heading & description (R2, packages/widgets/src/Widget.php:238). FormSection was missed by that sweep.

Not a duplicate: the only FormSection entry in the ledger is R2/line 37 (the Show/Hide collapse-toggle text), a different and already-fixed JS-side concern. The heading/description verbatim serialization lives in PHP Section.php and is not in any of rounds 1-6. Not a vendored shadcn primitive — Section.php is framework code and FormSection.tsx is under ui/src/form/, not ui/src/shadcn/.

Confidence high on is_real (exact analog already accepted as a bug; both ends confirmed by reading source). Duplicate=false confirmed by full ledger scan.

confirmed.title: Form Tab label serialized verbatim — never passed through __()
confirmed.detail: Tab::toArray() returns 'label' => $this->label with no __() resolution. FormTabs.tsx:92 renders {tab.label} raw. A tab label set as a translation key or English literal never localizes — same systemic serialization gap as Field/Column/Widget labels already in the ledger, but the form Tab layout component was not covered. Repro: Tab::make('details')->label(__('app.tabs.details')) under pt_BR → tab still shows the English/key string.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages/form/src/Layout/Tab.php:76: getTypeSpecificProps() returns 'label' => $this->label verbatim with no __() resolution. The label is stored raw in the constructor (line 29) and never localized at serialization. This is the same systemic PHP-label serialization gap already accepted as a real bug for Field (R1), Column (R1), Action (R1), Widget (R2), Resource (R3), NavigationGroup/Item (R3), and SelectField options (R6). User-facing: the tab label renders in the FormTabs UI. NOT a duplicate: scanned the full R1-R6 ledger — packages/form/src/Layout/Tab.php has zero entries; the form Tab/Tabs PHP layout component was never covered (the R2 FormSection.tsx entry is a different JS file). NOT a vendored shadcn primitive — it is framework code under packages/form/src/Layout/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Confidence is med rather than high because a label set via Tab::make('x', __('app.tabs.x')) resolves eagerly at construction (so that exact repro is wrong if the locale is already bound at definition time), but a label passed as a bare translation key or defined before locale binding never localizes — matching the precise failure mode accepted for the sibling components in the ledger.

confirmed.title: Wizard Step label serialized verbatim — never passed through __()
confirmed.detail: Step::toArray() emits 'label' => $this->getLabel(); getLabel() (line 89) returns the raw $this->label or a Str::title()-humanized name, with no __() call. WizardInput.tsx renders {step.label} raw (lines 491/503). A wizard step label is therefore never localized. The round-4 ledger entry covered fields-advanced editor *control* aria-labels but not the serialized step label itself. Repro: Step::make('profile')->label(__('app.wizard.profile')) under a non-English locale → step header shows the key/English text.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against source and ledger. Step::toArray() at packages/fields-advanced/src/Steps/Step.php:138 emits 'label' => $this->getLabel(); getLabel() (line 89) returns the raw $this->label or a Str::title()-humanized name from the step name, with no __() call anywhere in the file (no Lang/trans/__ import; only Str). WizardInput.tsx renders step.label verbatim at lines 491 and 503 (step headers) and builds the accessible/sr-only labels from it at lines 474 and 513. So a wizard step label is never localized through the framework, and the auto-humanized fallback is structurally untranslatable. This is a genuine, user-facing i18n defect matching the established confirmed class of 'PHP label serialized verbatim — never passed through __()' (Field.php R1, Column.php R1, Action.php R1, Widget.php R2, NavigationGroup/Item R3, InertiaDataBuilder Resource label R3, SelectField option labels R6). Novelty: no ledger entry references packages/fields-advanced/src/Steps/Step.php. The single fields-advanced ledger entry (R4, MarkdownInput.tsx:430) covers JS editor-control aria-labels, which is distinct from the serialized PHP step label. Not vendored: it is a framework PHP source file, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: Builder Block label serialized verbatim — never passed through __()
confirmed.detail: Block::toArray() emits 'label' => static::label() with no __() resolution; the React BuilderInput renders block labels raw in the add-block picker. A Block subclass returning a translation key or English label from label() never localizes. Same serialization gap family as Field/Column/Widget labels (ledger) but the builder Block was not swept. Repro: a Block whose label() returns __('app.blocks.hero') renders the key/English string in the block picker regardless of locale.
confirmed.why_real: CONFIRMED real, novel, user-facing i18n defect. Verified at packages/fields-advanced/src/Blocks/Block.php:78: `final toArray()` emits `'label' => static::label()` with no __()/trans()/Lang resolution anywhere in the file (grep for __/trans/Lang returned zero). The React consumer BuilderInput.tsx:450 renders `{block.label}` raw, with no t() wrap (contrast sibling aria-labels at lines 428/433 which DO use t()), so the PHP payload value reaches the add-block picker verbatim. A Block subclass returning label() => __('app.blocks.hero') or an English string renders that key/string regardless of active locale — genuine repro. NOT a duplicate: ledger has zero entries matching Blocks/Block or any 'block' label across R1-R6. This is the same serialization-gap FAMILY as the already-confirmed Field/Column/Widget/Nav/Select/Action label bugs (e.g. Field.php:189-203 was fixed to use trans($label)), but the builder Block was missed by every prior sweep — establishing parity that the fix is the same __()-wrap pattern and confirming this is a legitimate uncovered instance. NOT vendored: packages/fields-advanced/src/Blocks/ is first-party Arqel PHP, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: WizardInput per-step aria-label 'Step N: {label}' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: accessibleLabel = `Step ${idx + 1}: ${step.label}` hardcodes the English word 'Step' and is used both as the step button aria-label (line 483) and the inert step title attribute (line 500). The same component already uses t() for its Back/Next controls (line 570), so this is an inconsistency, not a missing hook. Screen-reader users on a non-English panel hear 'Step 1: …' in English. Not in the ledger (round 4 covered Markdown/editor control aria-labels, not the step-progress strings). Repro: switch panel to pt_BR, navigate a WizardField with a screen reader → step buttons announce 'Step 1' in English.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/wizard/WizardInput.tsx:474: `const accessibleLabel = \`Step ${idx + 1}: ${step.label}\`` hardcodes English "Step". It is consumed as the step button aria-label (line 483) and the inert step span title attribute (line 500). The component already imports and uses useArqelTranslations (t at line 304) and applies it for the Back/Next/Submit controls (lines 570/580/590), so this is a genuine localization inconsistency, not a missing-hook design gap. Git blame shows line 474 originates from the original component commit bd3c9cb and was NOT touched by the round-4 i18n sweep (2ce54e2), whose fields-advanced ledger entry only covers editor toolbar controls in MarkdownInput.tsx:430. No ledger entry (R1-R6) references WizardInput.tsx or these step-progress strings. The file lives in fields-advanced/src/wizard/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*, so it is not a vendored shadcn primitive. Reproduces: a screen-reader user on a pt_BR panel hears "Step 1: ..." in English while the navigation buttons announce in Portuguese. The same defect also affects the live region at line 513 ("Step N of M"), part of the same component finding.

confirmed.title: WizardInput sr-only progress 'Step N of M: {label}' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: The aria-live status region emits `Step ${safeIndex + 1} of ${total}: ${currentStep.label}` with hardcoded English 'Step'/'of'. Announced on every step transition. Not covered by the ledger. Repro: advance through a wizard under a non-English locale with a screen reader → the live region announces 'Step 2 of 3' in English.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/wizard/WizardInput.tsx:513: the aria-live="polite" role="status" region emits `Step ${safeIndex + 1} of ${total}: ${currentStep.label}` with hardcoded English "Step"/"of", announced on every wizard step transition. This is a genuine user-facing (screen-reader) i18n defect, consistent with already-confirmed sr-only live-region findings (e.g. CommandPalette R1, AI status R4). Not vendored — file is under fields-advanced/src/wizard/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not in the R1-R6 ledger: this file/line never appears; the only nearby fields-advanced entry is MarkdownInput.tsx:430 (R4), which covers editor-toolbar control aria-labels, a different surface and different file. Novel and reproduces.

confirmed.title: WizardInput empty-state 'No wizard steps configured.' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Empty-state text is a hardcoded English literal, not routed through t(), while sibling strings in the same file use t(). Repro: render a WizardField with zero steps under pt_BR → shows English 'No wizard steps configured.'
confirmed.why_real: Verified in /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-advanced/src/wizard/WizardInput.tsx. Line 436 renders the bare English literal "No wizard steps configured." as visible text content inside a <div> on the `if (total === 0)` empty-state branch (lines 429-438). The component already imports and uses the translation hook: line 304 `const t = useArqelTranslations();`, and sibling user-facing strings in the same file route through it (lines 570/580/590: t('arqel.fields_advanced.wizard_back/submit/next', ...)). The empty-state string is the only visible user-facing literal not passed through t(). It is reachable: a WizardField/WizardInput configured with zero steps (total===0) renders this branch, so under pt_BR the user sees English. Not vendored: the path is packages-js/fields-advanced/src/wizard/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: scanned the R1-R6 ledger (i18n-fixed-ledger.json) — the only fields-advanced entry is round 4 MarkdownInput.tsx:430 (editor control aria-labels); there is no entry for WizardInput.tsx nor for this empty-state string. Genuine, novel, user-facing missing-i18n defect.

confirmed.title: RepeaterInput visible '+ Add item' text and 'Remove item N' aria-label hardcoded
confirmed.detail: Line 633 renders the visible button text '+ Add item' as a hardcoded English literal even though the button's aria-label IS translated (line 631 uses t('arqel.fields_advanced.repeater_add_item')). Line 583 sets aria-label={`Remove item ${index + 1}`} hardcoded English. The round-4 sweep translated the add-button aria-label but left the visible label and the remove-button aria-label in English. Repro: under pt_BR, a RepeaterField shows a button reading '+ Add item' and each row's delete button announces 'Remove item 1' in English.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-advanced/src/repeater/RepeaterInput.tsx. Line 633 renders the visible button text '+ Add item' as a hardcoded English literal, while line 631's aria-label IS translated via t('arqel.fields_advanced.repeater_add_item', 'Add item') — so under pt_BR the screen reader announces the localized label but sighted users see English. Line 583 sets aria-label={`Remove item ${index + 1}`} as a hardcoded English template (line 573 similarly hardcodes 'Clone item N', further confirming the partial sweep). Both reproduce: a RepeaterField under pt_BR shows '+ Add item' and announces 'Remove item 1' in English. Not a duplicate: I grepped the entire ledger (/home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json) for repeater/RepeaterInput/'add item'/'remove item' — there is NO entry for RepeaterInput.tsx in any round. The only fields-advanced round-4 entry is MarkdownInput.tsx:430 ('fields-advanced editor controls: every aria-label hardcoded'), a different file covering the editor-type registration labels, not RepeaterInput's own add/remove buttons. Not vendored: this is framework-authored code under fields-advanced/src/repeater/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Genuine, novel, user-facing i18n defect.

confirmed.title: BuilderInput visible '+ Add block' text and 'Remove block N' aria-label hardcoded
confirmed.detail: Line 754 renders hardcoded English '+ Add block' visible text; line 701 sets aria-label={`Remove block ${index + 1}`} hardcoded. Same residual pattern as RepeaterInput — control aria-labels were partially translated in round 4 but these item-management strings were missed. Repro: under a non-English locale, BuilderField shows '+ Add block' and per-block delete announces 'Remove block 1' in English.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against packages-js/fields-advanced/src/builder/BuilderInput.tsx. Line 754 renders hardcoded English visible text `+ Add block`; line 701 sets aria-label={`Remove block ${index + 1}`} hardcoded English. The component imports useArqelTranslations (line 30) and uses t() actively in the same file (e.g. line 752 aria-label is translated via 'arqel.fields_advanced.builder_add_block'), confirming the infra exists and these strings were genuinely missed. Under a non-English locale the Add-block button shows English visible text and per-block delete announces 'Remove block N' in English -> real, user-facing defect. NOT vendored: file is framework source under fields-advanced/src/builder, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. NOT a duplicate: the sole fields-advanced ledger entry (round 4) targets MarkdownInput.tsx line 430 (editor-control aria-labels); no ledger entry covers BuilderInput.tsx's visible '+ Add block' text or 'Remove block N' aria-label. Minor inaccuracy in the finding: line 754 is the *visible text* that is hardcoded (the line-752 aria-label is actually translated), and the 'same as RepeaterInput' framing is loose; but the core defect (hardcoded visible add-block text + hardcoded remove-block aria-label) is accurate and reproduces.

confirmed.title: KeyValueInput visible '+ Add row' text and 'Remove row N' aria-label hardcoded
confirmed.detail: Line 295 renders hardcoded English '+ Add row' visible text; line 275 sets aria-label={`Remove row ${index + 1}`} hardcoded. (Line 293 also builds an aria-label by lowercasing keyLabel/valueLabel and joining with hardcoded English ' / … row' scaffolding.) KeyValueInput was not individually covered by the ledger. Repro: under pt_BR a KeyValueField shows '+ Add row' and each row delete announces 'Remove row 1' in English.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against the file. KeyValueInput.tsx line 295 renders the visible button text '+ Add row' as a hardcoded English string literal; line 275 sets aria-label={`Remove row ${index + 1}`} hardcoded; line 293 builds the add-button aria-label from hardcoded English scaffolding (`Add ${keyLabel.toLowerCase()} / ${valueLabel.toLowerCase()} row`). None pass through a translation hook (the component has no t()/useArqelTranslations usage for these strings). Under pt_BR a KeyValueField therefore shows '+ Add row' and announces 'Remove row N' in English — a genuine user-facing i18n defect. The file is packages-js/fields-advanced/src/key-value/KeyValueInput.tsx, NOT under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* (not a vendored shadcn primitive). Scanning the R1-R6 ledger, no entry references KeyValueInput.tsx or the key-value directory; the only fields-advanced entry is markdown/MarkdownInput.tsx (round 4), which is a different component. Novel, real, non-vendored, not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: RichTextInput max-length error message hardcoded English + un-grouped number
confirmed.detail: The over-limit alert renders `Content exceeds the maximum length of ${props.maxLength} characters.` as a hardcoded English sentence (role="alert"), not routed through t(), while the rest of the editor toolbar uses t() heavily. Additionally ${props.maxLength} is interpolated as a raw number with no Intl.NumberFormat grouping, so a 10000-char cap shows '10000' rather than the locale-grouped '10,000'/'10.000'. Repro: type past the limit under pt_BR → English error with un-grouped number.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/RichTextInput.tsx:384. The over-limit warning renders the literal template `Content exceeds the maximum length of ${props.maxLength} characters.` inside a role="alert" div, bypassing translation. The `t` translator (useArqelTranslations) is in scope at line 190 and used heavily in the same component (e.g. the toolbar aria-label at line 356), so this is a genuine, inconsistent missing-i18n defect that is user-facing: typing past maxLength under pt_BR surfaces English text. The secondary claim is also literally true — ${props.maxLength} is interpolated as a raw number with no Intl.NumberFormat grouping (10000 vs 10.000), though it is accessory to the primary defect. NOT a duplicate: the R1-R6 ledger contains no entry for RichTextInput.tsx; the nearest entry (R4) is markdown/MarkdownInput.tsx:430, a different file covering aria-labels, not this over-limit string. NOT vendored: the file is under packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: RichTextInput character counter 'N / M' emits un-grouped numbers (locale-insensitive)
confirmed.detail: The live character counter renders `{characters} / {props.maxLength}` as raw JS number→string, bypassing Intl.NumberFormat in the active locale. For large limits the grouping separator (',' vs '.' vs space) and digit shaping do not follow the viewer's locale. Repro: a field with maxLength 50000 shows '12345 / 50000' instead of the locale-formatted '12,345 / 50,000'.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/RichTextInput.tsx:393, which renders `{characters} / {props.maxLength}` via raw JS number-to-string interpolation, bypassing Intl.NumberFormat. For large maxLength (e.g. 50000) the live character counter shows '12345 / 50000' rather than the locale-formatted grouping ('12,345 / 50,000' or '12.345 / 50.000'), so grouping separators and digit shaping do not follow the viewer's locale — reproduces as described. Not vendored: the file is under fields-advanced/src, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: no R1-R6 ledger entry covers this file's character counter. The nearest number-format entries target different files (cells.tsx:169 NumberCell R2, CurrencyInput.tsx R3, AiImageInput.tsx:114 file-size R6), and the only fields-advanced/RichText ledger entries (markdown/MarkdownInput.tsx:430 and the RichText aria-label entry, R4) concern hardcoded aria-labels, not number formatting. This is consistent with the accepted ledger family of locale-insensitive number rendering (e.g. NumberCell ungrouped en-US grouping, R2). User-facing, novel, reproduces, not vendored. Confidence is med because the defect is marginal in severity — ungrouped digits in a character counter is a common and widely-tolerated UI convention — but it does match the precedent for what this ledger treats as an i18n defect.
